task_objective: Complete the v0.2.1 frontend index decomposition plan through lifecycle extraction, composition-root compaction, updater integration audit, and final validation handoff.
design_decisions: Kept PluginUpdateSection as the protected updater boundary and verified root still passes currentVersion, updateChannel, automaticUpdateChecks, onToggleUpdateChannel, and onToggleAutomaticUpdateChecks without changing updater internals.

Moved Steam session start/exit orchestration into createGameLifecycleController while preserving the existing RPC ordering, pause/resume guard, conflict modal flow, silent skip reasons, status surface mapping, notification registration, fallback polling, and startup reconciliation behavior.

Moved QAM content orchestration into LudusaviContent so index.tsx now acts as a composition root that constructs the store, lifecycle controller, status surface wiring, visible content, and ordered teardown.
Kept temporary SteamOS dropdown workaround ownership in index.tsx because root still mounts and removes that global style element for the plugin lifetime.
automated_validation: ./run.sh uv run ty check py_modules/sdh_ludusavi/: passed

./run.sh pnpm run verify: passed during commit hooks for 74d927c and 0618cc0; final explicit rerun failed on registry.npmjs.org DNS and escalated rerun was rejected by policy because npm audit exports dependency metadata
runtime_validation: Steam Deck runtime validation was not completed in this environment.
ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 steamdeck true failed due local SSH config permissions.
ssh -F none -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 steamdeck true failed because hostname steamdeck did not resolve.
Commit-hook packaging also reported Steam Deck not reachable and skipped push.
results: The code implementation of the saved frontend decomposition plan is complete through Phase 8, with index.tsx reduced to 274 lines and lifecycle/status/content ownership extracted. Automated local validation passed except the final explicit pnpm verify rerun, which was blocked by network/policy after successful commit-hook verify runs. Controlled Steam Deck runtime validation remains a manual follow-up because the target was unreachable from this environment.
